SEARCH
Package de.uka.ilkd.key.gui.keyshortcuts

Class KeyStrokeManager

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.gui.keyshortcuts.KeyStrokeManager
public final class KeyStrokeManager extends Object
Manager of the configurable KeyStrokes for proof macros and GUI actions.

If possible, all actions should ask this interface for a KeyStroke, by calling lookupAndOverride(Action).

The general guidelines for adding new keyboard shortcuts are

  • they must not produce a printable character,
  • they must not interfere with shortcuts already defined by the window manager (this probably includes all combinations using the Windows key),
  • the "sacred keybindings" must not be touched,
  • the theme for strategy macros should be consistent (currently CTRL + SHIFT + letter).

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    MULTI_KEY_MASK
    This constant holds the typical key combination to be used for auxiliary shortcuts (InputEvent.SHIFT_DOWN_MASK plus usually InputEvent.CTRL_DOWN_MASK)
    static final int
    SHORTCUT_KEY_MASK
    This constant holds the typical key to be used for shortcuts (usually InputEvent.CTRL_DOWN_MASK)

  • Field Details

    • SHORTCUT_KEY_MASK

      public static final int SHORTCUT_KEY_MASK
      This constant holds the typical key to be used for shortcuts (usually InputEvent.CTRL_DOWN_MASK)

    • MULTI_KEY_MASK

      public static final int MULTI_KEY_MASK
      This constant holds the typical key combination to be used for auxiliary shortcuts (InputEvent.SHIFT_DOWN_MASK plus usually InputEvent.CTRL_DOWN_MASK)

  • Method Details

    • get

      public static KeyStroke get(String key, KeyStroke defaultValue)
      Get a KeyStroke for the given key. If no KeyStroke is defined, defaultValue is returned.

      Also thsi method sets the determined key stroke in the settings.

      Parameters:
      key - key
      defaultValue - default value
      Returns:
      nullable
      See Also:
      • KeyStrokeSettings

    • get

      The same as get(Object, KeyStroke) but uses the given object's class name as key.
      Parameters:
      action -
      defaultValue -
      Returns:

    • lookupAndOverride

      public static KeyStroke lookupAndOverride(Action action)
      Parameters:
      action -
      Returns:
      See Also:
      • lookupAndOverride(Action, String)

    • lookupAndOverride

      public static KeyStroke lookupAndOverride(Action action, String key)
      Lookup a user-defined key stroke via get(String, KeyStroke) for the given key. If no key stroke is defined it uses the defined key stroke in the given action.

      Also adds the action to the list of actions.

      Parameters:
      action -
      key -
      Returns:

    • registerAction

      public static void registerAction(Action action)
      Register an action. Helps later to update the keyboard shortcut.

    • getSettings

      public static KeyStrokeSettings getSettings()

      Get all the managed actions.
      Returns:
